docs.title: Constraints and known limitations
docs.text:
• h5::read<std::string> does not compile — use h5::read<std::vector<std::string>>.
  • True scalar dataset round-trip (non-vector) is blocked on issue #89. Use std::vector<T>{value}.
  • All snippets require C++17.

docs.title: Include order and entry points
docs.text: h5cpp has three entry points. The choice depends on whether compiler-assisted struct reflection is needed.

docs.title: <h5cpp/all> — no reflection, simplest
docs.text:
Use when working with scalars, STL containers, and linalg types only.

#include <h5cpp/all>

Equivalent to core + io with no injection point. Any compiler-generated shim (h5cpp-compiler) cannot be sandwiched here.

docs.title: core → shim → io — with reflection (compiler plugin)
docs.text:
The include order is load-bearing. core builds the type/meta system. The compiler-generated shim specializes into it. io then sees the completed type registry.

#include <h5cpp/core>
#include "mystructs.h"   // compiler-generated shim from h5cpp-compiler
#include <h5cpp/io>

docs.title: Rules
docs.text:
• Never reverse core / io — io depends on core being complete.
  • H5Tmeta.hpp is the central trait file — only modify within the issue chain that owns the type system.
  • <h5cpp/all> and the core + io sandwich are mutually exclusive per translation unit.

docs.title: Minimal test include (used in all ctests)
docs.text:
#include <h5cpp/core>
#include <h5cpp/io>

docs.title: Streaming append / packet table
docs.text: h5::pt_t wraps a chunked extendable dataset for efficient streaming append. Elements accumulate in an internal buffer; the buffer flushes to disk when full or when h5::flush(pt) is called. The destructor flushes automatically.

docs.title: Setup — create the backing dataset
docs.text:
#include <h5cpp/all>

auto fd = h5::create("stream.h5", H5F_ACC_TRUNC);

// current_dims{0} = starts empty; max_dims{UNLIMITED} = grows without bound
auto ds = h5::create<int>(fd, "events",
    h5::current_dims{0},
    h5::max_dims{H5S_UNLIMITED},
    h5::chunk{1024});          // chunk size = flush granularity

h5::pt_t pt(ds);

docs.title: Append scalars
docs.text:
for (int i = 0; i < 25; ++i)
    h5::append(pt, i);

h5::flush(pt);   // write remaining buffer to disk

docs.title: Append a vector chunk
docs.text:
std::vector<int> chunk = {1, 2, 3, 4, 5};
h5::append(pt, chunk);
h5::flush(pt);

docs.title: Append strings
docs.text:
auto ds = h5::create<std::string>(fd, "labels",
    h5::current_dims{0},
    h5::max_dims{H5S_UNLIMITED},
    h5::chunk{128});
h5::pt_t pt(ds);

h5::append(pt, std::string{"alpha"});
h5::append(pt, "beta");    // const char* also accepted
h5::flush(pt);

docs.title: Flush on destruction (RAII)
docs.text:
{
    h5::pt_t pt(ds);
    for (int i = 0; i < 7; ++i)
        h5::append(pt, i);
    // destructor calls flush() — partial chunk written with fill value
}

docs.title: 2D streaming (fixed inner extent)
docs.text:
// Shape: rows grow, columns fixed at 5
auto ds = h5::create<int>(fd, "matrix_stream",
    h5::current_dims{0, 5},
    h5::max_dims{H5S_UNLIMITED, 5},
    h5::chunk{64, 5});
h5::pt_t pt(ds);

std::vector<int> row = {1, 2, 3, 4, 5};
h5::append(pt, row);     // appends one row
h5::flush(pt);

docs.title: Key invariants
docs.text:
• Chunk size sets the write granularity — too small → many small writes; too large → large unflushed buffer. Tune to expected event rate × element size.
  • A partial final chunk is written with the dataset's fill value on flush.
  • h5::pt_t is copyable (shares the underlying dataset handle).
  • Do not mix h5::append and h5::write on the same dataset handle.

docs.title: File create
docs.text:
#include <h5cpp/all>

// Create (truncate if exists) — returns RAII fd_t, closes on scope exit
auto fd = h5::create("data.h5", H5F_ACC_TRUNC);

// Open existing
auto fd = h5::open("data.h5", H5F_ACC_RDONLY);
auto fd = h5::open("data.h5", H5F_ACC_RDWR);

h5::fd_t is move-only. H5Fclose is called automatically on destruction.

docs.title: File create with property lists
docs.text:
auto fd = h5::create("data.h5", H5F_ACC_TRUNC,
    h5::file_space_page_size{4096} | h5::userblock{512});

docs.title: Dataset create — fixed dimensions
docs.text:
// Type is the template parameter. Dimensions via current_dims.
auto ds = h5::create<double>(fd, "/results/matrix",
    h5::current_dims{10, 20});

docs.title: Dataset create — chunked, extendable, compressed
docs.text:
auto ds = h5::create<short>(fd, "/sensor/raw",
    h5::current_dims{10, 20},
    h5::max_dims{10, H5S_UNLIMITED},
    h5::chunk{2, 3} | h5::gzip{9} | h5::shuffle | h5::fletcher32);
  • h5::max_dims{..., H5S_UNLIMITED} marks an extendable dimension.
  • Chunk must be set whenever H5S_UNLIMITED appears in max_dims.
  • h5::shuffle before h5::gzip improves compression ratio for numeric data.

docs.title: Dataset create — packet table (streaming)
docs.text:
// current_dims{0} + max_dims{UNLIMITED} + chunk = packet table layout
auto ds = h5::create<int>(fd, "/stream/events",
    h5::current_dims{0},
    h5::max_dims{H5S_UNLIMITED},
    h5::chunk{1024});

See io-append.md for the corresponding h5::append usage.

docs.title: Create with path auto-creation (lcpl)
docs.text:
// Creates intermediate groups automatically
auto ds = h5::create<float>(fd, "/a/b/c/dataset",
    h5::current_dims{100},
    h5::create_path | h5::utf8);   // lcpl

docs.title: Handle types
docs.text:
Type Meaning h5::fd_t file descriptor — wraps hid_t from H5Fcreate / H5Fopen h5::ds_t dataset descriptor — wraps hid_t from H5Dcreate / H5Dopen h5::pt_t packet table — wraps ds_t for streaming append

All are RAII — do not call H5Fclose / H5Dclose manually on managed handles.

docs.title: Full read — returns owned object
docs.text:
#include <h5cpp/all>

auto fd = h5::open("data.h5", H5F_ACC_RDONLY);

auto v      = h5::read<std::vector<double>>(fd, "sensor/temperature");
auto labels = h5::read<std::vector<std::string>>(fd, "labels");
auto M      = h5::read<arma::mat>(fd, "results/matrix");

The template argument is the target C++ type. h5cpp handles all allocation.

docs.title: Read into pre-allocated buffer (high-performance loops)
docs.text:
// Open dataset once, outside the loop
auto ds = h5::open(fd, "sensor/temperature");
std::vector<double> buf(1024);

for (...) {
    h5::read(ds, buf.data(), h5::offset{i * 1024}, h5::count{1024});
}

Pass a raw pointer + h5::count when the buffer is pre-allocated. This avoids allocation on every iteration.

docs.title: Partial read — hyperslab selection
docs.text:
auto ds  = h5::open(fd, "results/matrix");
auto row = h5::read<arma::rowvec>(ds, h5::offset{3, 0}, h5::count{1, 20});

docs.title: Read via file path (without opening fd separately)
docs.text: auto M = h5::read<arma::mat>("data.h5", "results/matrix");

docs.title: Type constraints
docs.text:
Want Use std::string (single) h5::read<std::vector<std::string>> — returns vector of size 1 scalar int h5::read<std::vector<int>> — round-trip scalars blocked on #89 matrix h5::read<arma::mat> / h5::read<Eigen::MatrixXd> etc.

h5::read<std::string> does not compile. The decay path routes std::string into the generic read (not the string specialization). Always use std::vector<std::string>.

docs.title: Error on missing dataset
docs.text:
// Throws h5::error::io::dataset::open
h5::read<std::vector<int>>(fd, "nonexistent");

Use h5::mute() / h5::unmute() around expected failures to suppress HDF5's CAPI error output.

docs.title: One-shot write — creates dataset and writes in one call
docs.text:
#include <h5cpp/all>

auto fd = h5::create("data.h5", H5F_ACC_TRUNC);

std::vector<double> v = {1.0, 2.0, 3.0};
h5::write(fd, "sensor/temperature", v);   // dataset created from v's size

std::vector<std::string> labels = {"alpha", "beta", "gamma"};
h5::write(fd, "labels", labels);

docs.title: Write to an existing dataset handle
docs.text:
auto ds = h5::open(fd, "sensor/temperature");
h5::write(ds, v);

docs.title: Partial write — offset and stride into an existing dataset
docs.text:
// Dataset must already exist with sufficient dimensions
arma::mat M(2, 3); M.ones();
h5::write(ds, M, h5::offset{2, 2}, h5::stride{1, 3});

docs.title: Write with explicit current dimensions (creates dataset)
docs.text:
// Places a 1D vector as a row inside a 2D dataset
std::vector<double> v(8);
h5::write(fd, "matrix_slice", v,
    h5::current_dims{40, 50},
    h5::offset{5, 0},
    h5::count{1, 1},
    h5::stride{3, 5},
    h5::block{2, 4},
    h5::max_dims{40, H5S_UNLIMITED});

docs.title: Known constraints
docs.text:
• h5::write(fd, "ds", 42) — scalar write creates H5S_SCALAR space. Round-tripping via h5::read is blocked on #89. Use a 1-element vector.
  • Strings: write as std::vector<std::string>, not std::string.
  • h5::offset, h5::stride, h5::count, h5::block arguments may appear in any order after the data argument.

docs.title: Property list composition
docs.text: h5cpp property lists are composed with operator|. All properties in a chain must be the same list type (dcpl, fapl, lcpl, etc.) — mixing types is a compile-time error.

docs.title: Dataset creation properties (dcpl)
docs.text:
// Chunk + compression pipeline (most common)
h5::dcpl_t dcpl = h5::chunk{2, 3}
                | h5::shuffle           // reorder bytes for better compression
                | h5::gzip{9}           // deflate level 0–9
                | h5::fletcher32;       // checksum

// Fill value
h5::dcpl_t dcpl = h5::chunk{64} | h5::fill_value<short>{42};

// All filters in one expression (passed directly to create)
auto ds = h5::create<short>(fd, "dataset",
    h5::current_dims{10, 20},
    h5::max_dims{10, H5S_UNLIMITED},
    h5::chunk{2, 3} | h5::gzip{6} | h5::shuffle);

docs.title: Available dcpl properties
docs.text: Property Effect h5::chunk{n} / h5::chunk{n,m} enable chunked layout, set chunk shape h5::gzip{level} deflate compression, level 0–9 h5::shuffle byte-shuffle filter (improves gzip ratio for numerics) h5::nbit n-bit filter for integer types h5::fletcher32 Fletcher-32 checksum h5::fill_value<T>{v} default fill value for unwritten elements h5::alloc_time{H5D_ALLOC_TIME_EARLY} allocation timing h5::fill_time{H5D_FILL_TIME_ALLOC} fill timing h5::layout{H5D_CHUNKED} explicit layout (usually inferred from chunk)

docs.title: File access properties (fapl)
docs.text: h5::fapl_t fapl = h5::fclose_degree_weak | h5::stdio;

docs.title: File creation properties (fcpl)
docs.text:
h5::fcpl_t fcpl = h5::file_space_page_size{4096} | h5::userblock{512};
auto fd = h5::create("data.h5", H5F_ACC_TRUNC, fcpl);

docs.title: Link creation properties (lcpl)
docs.text:
// Auto-create intermediate groups + UTF-8 link names
h5::lcpl_t lcpl = h5::create_path | h5::utf8;

// Pass lcpl to create
auto ds = h5::create<float>(fd, "/a/b/c/dataset",
    h5::current_dims{100}, lcpl);

h5::create_path and h5::utf8 are pre-built lcpl_t instances — they are or'd directly without constructing a named object:

auto ds = h5::create<float>(fd, "/a/b/c/ds",
    h5::current_dims{100},
    h5::create_path | h5::utf8,   // lcpl inline
    h5::chunk{32} | h5::gzip{6}); // dcpl inline

docs.title: Operator|= (in-place composition)
docs.text:
h5::dcpl_t dcpl = h5::chunk{16} | h5::gzip{4};
dcpl |= h5::fletcher32;   // add to existing list

docs.title: Default property lists
docs.text:
Symbol Meaning h5::default_lcpl create_path \| utf8 h5::default_dapl H5P_DEFAULT dataset access h5::default_fapl H5P_DEFAULT file access

Pass these explicitly when you need a placeholder:

auto ds = h5::create<short>(fd, "ds",
    h5::current_dims{10},
    h5::default_lcpl,
    h5::chunk{4} | h5::gzip{3},
    h5::default_dapl);
